pattern: The smallest surviving value must be a shifted-down copy of the smallest recovered value, so trying each plausible shift and greedily pairing values from the bottom up either completes or rules that shift out.
hints: Whatever the hidden shift is, the very smallest surviving number has to be the smallest recovered value shifted down, which narrows down what the shift could be.
Sort the surviving numbers. The smallest one, paired with some other entry exactly twice-the-shift above it, tells you a candidate shift; only a few candidates are worth trying.

For each candidate shift k (built from the smallest value and every other value that could be its shifted-up partner), repeatedly take the smallest remaining number, require its exact partner at value + 2k to still be available, remove both, and record their midpoint; if every number gets consumed this way, that shift produced a valid recovered array.

edge_inputs.description: smallest possible size, a single recovered value
shift produces an overlapping value shared between the low and high halves
values near the top of the allowed range
repeated recovered values create repeated survivors on both sides
consecutive recovered values make the low and high halves overlap in the middle
smallest surviving value touches the minimum allowed value
two duplicated recovered values with a larger shift to rule out smaller false candidates

stress.description: large random valid instances built by choosing a shift and array first, then shuffling their combined survivors
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(1000, size // 2))
    k = rng.randrange(1, 1000)
    arr = [rng.randrange(k + 1, 10**9 - k) for _ in range(n)]
    nums = []
    for v in arr:
        nums.append(v - k)
        nums.append(v + k)
    rng.shuffle(nums)
    return [nums]
